claims.claim: Telegram released Bot API v10.0 in April 2026, adding guest-bot multi-agent flows on top of v9.6 features (Managed Bots, Stars subscriptions, Gifts API, Bot Business mode, Mini Apps 2.0).
Telegram publishes official Bot API release notes on the BotNews Telegram channel at t.me/botnews.
grammY (JavaScript/TypeScript Telegram bot framework) documentation is at grammy.dev.
Telegraf (JavaScript Telegram bot framework, predecessor of grammY) documentation is at telegraf.js.org.
Telegram's default Bot API server limits bot file uploads to 50 MB; running a local Bot API server raises the limit to 2 GB for premium bots.

Telegram's REST Bot API endpoint pattern is api.telegram.org/bot<token>/<method>; the canonical endpoint for registering webhooks is the setWebhook method. Example webhook URLs in the skill (your-domain.com, your-app.vercel.app, your-app.com, example.com) are placeholders the user must replace with their real deployment host.
claims.raw_snippet: api.telegram.org https://api.telegram.org/bot${BOT_TOKEN}/setWebhook?url=https://your-app.vercel.app/api/webhook&secret_token=${SECRET} https://api.telegram.org/bot<token https://example.com/webhook https://your-app.com/mini-app https://your-domain.com https://your-domain.com/webhook https://your-domain.com/webhook/${process.env.WEBHOOK_SECRET}

claims.claim: BotFather is Telegram's official bot management bot; the /newbot command creates a new bot and /revokentoken regenerates the token of an existing one.
systemd unit files for Telegram bot deployments are conventionally placed at /etc/systemd/system/telegram-bot.service following the standard systemd directory layout.
Telegram Bot Business mode (introduced in 2024) is documented at core.telegram.org/bots/business and Mini Apps / WebApp at core.telegram.org/bots/webapps.
Telegram Mini Apps (WebApp) documentation is at core.telegram.org/bots/webapps.
The grammy-transformer-throttler middleware (github.com/grammyjs/transformer-throttler) enforces Telegram's rate limits in grammY-based bots.
Telegram's self-hostable local Bot API server (allowing the 2 GB upload limit) is published at github.com/tdlib/telegram-bot-api.
